title: Page Help Library — rich-content help documents per addressable
scope:
The current pane-help system (PLA-0004) stores a single body_html per addressable. The hexagon icon on every <Panel> renders that body in a small popover. That model breaks down the moment a help entry needs more than a paragraph: multi-step user guides, embedded video walkthroughs, screenshots, deep-dive reference. This plan extends the substrate into a real page help library:

  • A doc carries a title, a rich body (sanitised HTML), an ordered list of YouTube embed URLs, and an ordered list of external image URLs.
  • Every addressable that registers via useRegisterAddressable gets a help-doc auto-seeded on first mount — no panel ever ships unhelpable.
  • The hexagon popover stays as the at-a-glance preview; a new /help/<addressable_id> route renders the doc full-width for deep reading.
  • Gadmins edit docs in DevPageHelpPanel via a rich content editor with live preview that mirrors the popover render.
  • A shared HTML sanitiser + YouTube whitelist sits between every write and the database; XSS payloads and non-YouTube iframes never reach storage.
  • The <makeapp> / <makedevapp> skills now POST a stub doc on scaffold with an AI-suggested placeholder body so the new app's help is pre-populated.
value:
Three audiences, three wins:

  • Users: every panel becomes self-explanatory; the hexagon stops being a 'who-knows-what's-here' button and becomes a real help affordance with video walk-throughs.
  • Gadmins: can author rich help in-product without filing a deploy ticket; live preview matches what users see; sanitiser keeps them from accidentally shipping XSS.
  • Devs: stops the 'what does this panel do again?' loop. Auto-seed + skill update means the help doc is part of the scaffold contract, not an afterthought.

The substrate also positions us for the eventual i18n migration (R033) — a single rich-content table per addressable is far easier to translate than English-strings-scattered-across-React.
implementation_plan: Migration 093: extend page_help with title, video_embeds JSONB, image_urls JSONB, updated_by, updated_at (story 00323).
Build htmlsan util with SanitiseHTML + ValidateYouTubeURL, exhaustive XSS test corpus (story 00330).
Backend page_help service: GET full doc + gadmin-gated PUT, both routed through htmlsan (story 00324).
Auto-seed wiring: addressables.Service.Register triggers placeholder upsert in page_help; idempotent (story 00325).
Frontend: refactor Panel popover to render full doc (title + sanitised body + whitelisted YouTube iframes + image gallery + 'Open full page' link) (story 00326).
Frontend: new /help/[addressable_id] route — full-width doc renderer; auth-required; 404 on unknown id (story 00327).
Frontend: gadmin editor in DevPageHelpPanel — body textarea + repeating YouTube/image inputs + live preview matching popover (story 00328).
Skill update: <makeapp>/<makedevapp> POST a stub doc with AI-inferred placeholder body on scaffold (story 00329).
Integration suite: register → seed → gadmin PUT → popover render → /help route render; negative paths for 403, sanitiser, whitelist (story 00331).
areas_impacted: UI: app/components/Panel.tsx (popover refactor to render rich doc).
GOV: dev/pages/DevPageHelpPanel.tsx (rich content editor + live preview).
feature_list: Rich help doc model: title + sanitised HTML body + YouTube embeds + image URLs + audit fields.
Auto-seed on first addressable register — every panel ships with a discoverable, editable help row.
Hexagon popover renders the full rich doc inline, with safe YouTube iframes and image gallery.
Full-page /help/<addressable_id> route for deep-reading help (bookmarkable, SEO meta from title).
Gadmin in-product editor with live preview that exactly matches the popover render.
Server-side HTML sanitiser + YouTube URL whitelist on every write path.
Skill-driven placeholder seeding so new panels never ship help-less.
features_extended: PLA-0004 pane_help: extended from single body_html to full rich-content doc.
PLA-0005 addressables.Service.Register: extended to trigger auto-seed of help-doc placeholder.
<makeapp>/<makedevapp> skills: extended to POST a stub help doc on scaffold.

work_item_backlog.title: Migration: extend page_help with rich-content columns
Backend: page_help full-doc CRUD service (GET + gadmin PUT)
Backend: auto-seed help-doc stub on first addressable register
Frontend: refactor Panel hexagon popover to render rich help content
Gadmin editor in DevPageHelpPanel — rich content + live preview
Skill update: <makeapp> + <makedevapp> POST stub help doc on scaffold
Backend: HTML sanitisation + YouTube URL whitelist library
Tests: integration suite covering register → seed → edit → render lifecycle

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Migration 093 applies cleanly and adds title, video_embeds JSONB, image_urls JSONB, updated_by, updated_at to page_help.
acceptance_criteria.proven_by: psql migration apply succeeds; \d page_help shows the new columns with correct types and defaults.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Existing seeded page_help rows survive the migration with body_html unchanged.
acceptance_criteria.proven_by: SELECT count(*) and SELECT body_html before/after match for the 5 PLA-0004 seed rows.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: GET /api/page-help/{addressable_id} returns the full rich doc.
acceptance_criteria.proven_by: Response body matches schema {title, body_html, video_embeds[], image_urls[], updated_by, updated_at}; 404 on unknown id.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: PUT /api/page-help/{addressable_id} requires gadmin and runs all input through htmlsan.
acceptance_criteria.proven_by: Non-gadmin caller receives 403; gadmin write persists sanitised HTML; YouTube URL failing the whitelist returns 400.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Auto-seed fires on first addressable register and is idempotent.
acceptance_criteria.proven_by: Register POST for unseen addressable_id creates page_help row with placeholder body; second register leaves the row (and any edits) unchanged.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Panel popover renders sanitised body + whitelisted YouTube iframes + image gallery + Open-full-page link.
acceptance_criteria.proven_by: Visual test: dangerous tags stripped; YouTube embeds appear only for whitelist matches; non-whitelist URLs silently dropped; link navigates to /help/{id}.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: /help/{addressable_id} renders the full doc full-width with auth required.
acceptance_criteria.proven_by: Page renders title as h1, sanitised body, full-size videos, image gallery; unknown id returns 404; anonymous request redirects to /login.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Gadmin editor renders pre-populated, validates YouTube URLs on blur, and live preview matches popover render exactly.
acceptance_criteria.proven_by: Edit button opens editor with current values; bad YouTube URL shows inline error and blocks save; preview output identical to popover render in story 00326.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: <makeapp> and <makedevapp> seed a stub help doc with AI-inferred placeholder on scaffold.
acceptance_criteria.proven_by: Running each skill creates a page_help row tied to the new app's addressable_id; placeholder body mentions the app name and a one-paragraph inferred purpose.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: htmlsan.SanitiseHTML strips a 20+ payload OWASP XSS corpus.
acceptance_criteria.proven_by: Unit tests pass for every payload; outputs free of script/style/event-handler attributes.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: htmlsan.ValidateYouTubeURL accepts only the documented whitelist.
acceptance_criteria.proven_by: Unit tests: youtube.com/embed/{id}, youtube.com/watch?v={id}, youtu.be/{id} all canonicalise; youtube.evil.com, vimeo.com, javascript:, data: all return ErrInvalidYouTubeURL.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: End-to-end suite covers the full register → seed → edit → render lifecycle and the negative paths.
acceptance_criteria.proven_by: npm run test:e2e -- page-help-library passes green; suite includes 403-on-non-gadmin, sanitiser-strips-script, and YouTube-whitelist-rejects-vimeo cases.

risks.risk: XSS via untrusted HTML in body_html. The popover ships on every page in the product — a single stored XSS would have site-wide blast radius.
risks.mitigation: Story 00330 builds a shared sanitiser used by every write path (PUT handler + auto-seed). Test corpus includes the OWASP XSS cheat-sheet payloads. Sanitiser runs server-side, not just render-side.

risks.risk: YouTube URL spoofing — an attacker enters a youtube-look-alike URL (youtube.evil.com, youtube.com.evil/, etc.) that the whitelist accidentally accepts.
risks.mitigation: ValidateYouTubeURL parses the URL, checks the host is exactly one of the three documented forms (youtube.com, www.youtube.com, youtu.be), and returns the canonical /embed/ form. Anything else returns ErrInvalidYouTubeURL. Test cases cover the look-alike attack.

risks.risk: Auto-seed write storm — every panel mount POSTs a register call; if the seed path is not idempotent, a busy app could insert thousands of duplicate rows or hammer the DB.
risks.mitigation: Story 00325 implements seed via INSERT ... ON CONFLICT (addressable_id) DO NOTHING. Service test asserts that a re-register leaves the existing row (and any gadmin edits) untouched.

risks.risk: Live preview drifts from popover render — gadmin authors something that looks fine in the editor but renders broken to users.
risks.mitigation: The editor's preview pane imports the same render component the popover uses; story 00328 AC explicitly tests the preview/popover equality.

risks.risk: Existing seeded pane_help rows from PLA-0004 break under the new schema.
risks.mitigation: Migration 093 is additive only — no column drops, no NOT NULL on new columns; defaults provided. Story 00323 AC verifies before/after row count and body_html parity.

references.label: PLA-0005 — addressables substrate (Register hook for auto-seed)
